title: Degraded Verdict Trajectory Entry
description: Schema for trajectory log entries emitted by degraded-verdict-lib.sh (cycle-117 item D, issue #1177) whenever a gate writer (adversarial-review.sh, red-team-code-vs-design.sh, flatline-orchestrator.sh) produces a non-APPROVED verdict band. A fully-clean run never produces a record — the emit helper (degraded_verdict_maybe_emit) no-ops for APPROVED/UNKNOWN bands, so verdict_band is restricted to DEGRADED/FAILED here by construction, not by this schema alone.

gate.description: Identifies the writer and sub-mode that produced this record, e.g. "adversarial-review:audit", "adversarial-review:review", "red-team:code-vs-design", "flatline:prd" (flatline has no numbered sprint id, so $phase — prd/sdd/sprint — is the closest per-invocation identifier and is embedded here, not in sprint_id).

verdict_band.description: Never APPROVED or UNKNOWN — the emit helper no-ops for those bands so no record is ever written for a clean run.

degradation_reason.description: Free-text cause, e.g. "model_invocation_failed", "empty_or_invalid_model_output", or a voices_dropped[].reason value from the verdict-quality aggregator (EmptyContent, Other, etc.).

degraded_legs.description: Model/voice identifiers that dropped or failed on this gate invocation. Omitted entirely (not an empty array) when the writer has no leg identifiers available.

model_exit_code.description: Process exit code of the failing model invocation. null when no single exit code applies (e.g. an aggregate multi-voice DEGRADED with no failing exit captured). flatline's writer is lossy here by design: when more than one voice drops, only the FIRST dropped voice's exit_code survives this scalar field (the issue's schema has one field, not a per-leg array) — a known v1 limitation, not a bug.

sprint_id.description: Sprint identifier for adversarial-review/red-team gates; flatline has no numbered sprint, so its $phase (prd/sdd/sprint) is passed here instead.

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the record was emitted.
